column1: # Unified curated and Vahan-bundle event timeline; possible_overlap flags collisions.

column9: detail
column10: source

column8: Global Financial Crisis — India Credit Tightening
column9: Global Financial Crisis impact on India: credit tightening, export demand collapse. All-segment vehicle demand dip Q4 FY09 through Q2 FY10. Pre-Vahan4 era — data quality note: VAHAN data coverage was less complete before 2012.
column10: RBI FSR 2009; SIAM annual report FY09

column9: BS3 emission norms extended to 2-wheelers and 3-wheelers nationwide from April 2010. Front-load spike in March 2010 as dealers cleared BS2 two-wheeler inventory. CNG 3W also affected. Signal mainly in Petrol 2W chart.
column10: MoRTH BS3 notification Oct 2010; SIAM FY10 data

column9: National Electric Mobility Mission Plan target of 6-7M xEV by 2020
column10: MoHI

column9: Ola (2010) and Uber (2013) scaled aggressively 2014–2016. Commercial cab demand drove CNG and Diesel Motor Cab registrations up multi-fold. Effect strong in AP Tier-2 cities (Vizag, Vijayawada) from 2015. Reversed from 2018 as driver incomes collapsed and aggregator growth slowed.
column10: FADA motor cab data 2014-18; Ola/Uber fleet registration data; SIAM

column9: Telangana separated from Andhra Pradesh on June 2, 2014. Hyderabad + Rangareddy + Medchal RTOs (accounting for ~45–53% of undivided AP registrations) transferred to Telangana. AP Vahan data shows a structural ~50% step-down from this date. THIS IS NOT A DEMAND EVENT. All YoY comparisons across June 2014 in AP data are invalid.
column10: Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act 2014; Vahan RTO count change Jun 2014; FADA AP state data

column9: Full market pricing of diesel; narrowed petrol-diesel gap eroded diesel PV share
column10: MoPNG

column9: Cabinet fully deregulated diesel prices in October 2014. Diesel price parity with petrol reached by 2015. Diesel's historical 40–60% cost advantage over petrol eroded. Structural context for Diesel's long-term demand decline from 2015 onward — accelerated later by BS6.
column10: MoPNG Oct 2014; PPAC price data FY15

column8: FAME I Launched — Early EV Stimulation
column9: FAME I (Faster Adoption of Manufacturing of EVs) launched April 2015 with ₹895 Cr over 4 years. Signal is small in All India aggregate (EV was <0.1% of market) but visible in EV-specific chart as growth baseline. LOW CONFIDENCE annotation — only relevant for EV fuel-type chart, not visible in Petrol/Diesel views.
column10: MHI FAME-I notification Apr 2015; SMEV data FY16; PIB

column9: Demand incentives for xEVs incl. PV
column10: MoHI

column8: Delhi NGT Diesel Ban — Localised Effect
column9: National Green Tribunal banned diesel vehicles above 2000cc in Delhi-NCR (Dec 2015, extended). Luxury diesel SUV segment in Delhi effectively killed. Effect localised to NCR; minimal in All India aggregate or AP data.
column10: NGT order Dec 2015; Delhi HC; Supreme Court Jan 2016 confirmation

column9: Supreme Court temporary ban on large diesel PV registrations in NCR (till Aug 2016)
column10: Supreme Court

column9: BS4 emission norms extended to 2-wheelers nationwide from April 2016. March 2016 shows elevated 2W Petrol registrations (front-load) followed by norm cliff in April 2016. Less severe than 4W transitions due to smaller price differential.
column10: MoRTH BS4 notification; SIAM 2W monthly data Mar-Apr 2016

column9: PM Modi demonetised ₹500/₹1000 notes on Nov 8, 2016. Impact specific to cash-intensive segments: 2-wheelers (₹50k–80k price, often cash purchases) and rural buyers. 4W car registrations (credit/EMI-financed) showed minimal impact. AP rural areas show stronger signal than metro areas. Q4 FY17 2W Petrol dip ~15% in AP.
column10: GOI notification Nov 8 2016; SIAM Nov-Dec 2016; FADA; RBI currency report

column9: Cash crunch hit auto retail H2 FY17
column10: RBI

column8: VAHAN 4.0 Platform Migration — Data Quality Event
column9: VAHAN 4.0 platform migration in 2017 may have caused methodology changes in how registrations are counted and attributed. Any anomaly in 2017 data should be cross-referenced against this data quality event before attributing to policy causes. SIAM wholesale data is the preferred cross-check for this period.
column10: MoRTH VAHAN 4.0 launch announcement; NIC; SIAM

column9: Supreme Court ordered ban on BS3 vehicle registrations with only 2 days notice (order March 29, effective April 1, 2017). Dealers were left with BS3 inventory they could not register. This creates an OEM-level cliff — particularly visible in Maker-dimension data for OEMs with high BS3 diesel inventory. No demand signal — this is a supply/compliance event.
column10: Supreme Court order Mar 29 2017; SIAM emergency advisory; FADA Apr 2017

column9: Apr–Jul 2017 contains three overlapping events: BS4 national rollout (Apr 1), pre-GST buy rush (June — dealers clearing old inventory), and GST launch disruption (July 1). Registration patterns in this 4-month window reflect simultaneous norm transition and tax reform. Any anomaly in this window may be attributed to either or both. Cannot be cleanly separated.
column10: GST Council Jun 2017; MoRTH BS4 notification; SIAM FADA data Apr-Jul 2017

column9: Pre-buy and inventory effects around enforcement
column10: MoRTH

column8: Pre-GST Buy Rush + GST Launch Disruption
column9: June 2017: dealers cleared old inventory before GST launch — front-load spike especially in Diesel and large-car segments where GST cess increases were steepest. July 2017: dealers recalibrated invoicing under new GST system, causing 2–3 week paralysis. Composite signal — part of the broader BS4/GST Apr–Jul 2017 window.
column10: SIAM Jun-Jul 2017; GST Council; FADA monthly retail Jul 2017

column9: 28% + cess on cars replaced VAT/excise; relative price shifts across segments
column10: GST Council

column8: GST Cess on Diesel 4W — 50% Effective Rate
column9: GST cess structure: large diesel cars attracted 28% GST + 22% cess = 50% effective rate. This was materially higher than pre-GST incidence. Mid and large diesel SUV segment directly impacted. Reinforces the BS4/GST composite window annotation (A05).
column10: GST Council rate schedule Jul 2017; SIAM GST impact analysis

column8: CNG CGD 9th Round Expansion — National CNG Growth
column9: PNGRB 9th City Gas Distribution bidding round (2018) awarded CNG distribution rights to 86 new districts. CNG station network expanded from ~1,400 in 2018 to ~5,000+ by 2022. This supply-side infrastructure expansion directly drove CNG vehicle registration growth — more cities made CNG viable for buyers. AP benefitted late (CNG infrastructure lagged national average in AP).
column10: PNGRB CGD rounds data; PPAC CGD expansion report 2021; SIAM CNG data

column8: Ola/Uber Driver Income Collapse — Cab Demand Cliff
column9: Aggregator driver incomes collapsed 2018 as Ola/Uber cut incentives. New driver-partner registrations slowed significantly. Motor Cab (CNG and Diesel) demand dipped after the 2014–2017 surge. Effect visible in Motor Cab class registrations in AP Tier-2 cities (Vizag, Vijayawada).
column10: IDC driver survey 2018; FADA Motor Cab class data; SIAM

column8: AP EV Policy 2018 — 100% Road Tax Waiver
column9: Andhra Pradesh EV Policy 2018–2023 offered 100% road tax exemption for EVs. Road tax on an ₹8L EV can be ₹50,000–80,000 — a material cost reduction. AP EV registrations grew faster than national average from FY2019 onward. Signal strongest in EV 2W and EV 3W (most price-sensitive). AP punches above its income level on EV adoption due to this policy.
column10: AP Govt GO Ms No. 5 Feb 2018; SMEV AP data; EV Reporter AP analysis

column9: MoRTH relaxed axle load norms by 20% in July 2018, effectively allowing trucks to carry more cargo per trip. Reduced demand for incremental trucks in the short term (existing fleet became more efficient). Context for Diesel CV demand trajectory mid-2018.
column10: MoRTH GVW notification Jul 2018; SIAM CV data Q2 FY19

column9: IL&FS default in September 2018 triggered an NBFC liquidity crisis. Auto financing dried up for 2-wheelers, 3-wheelers, and commercial vehicles. SIAM reports FY2019 vehicle sales declined 15% YoY. Effect was sustained for 6 months (Q3 FY19 through Q4 FY19). AP shows stronger signal due to above-average auto-rickshaw (CNG 3W) density.
column10: RBI FSR Dec 2018; SIAM FY19 annual data; FADA; CRISIL credit report

column9: Auto retail financing squeeze FY19-FY20
column10: RBI

column8: FAME I Ends — Gap Before FAME II
column9: FAME I ended March 2019. Brief gap before FAME II (Apr 2019). Minor EV 2W dip visible only in EV-specific chart. Contextual: explains why EV 2W shows minor softness in Feb–Mar 2019 before the FAME II surge.
column10: MHI FAME-I closure; SMEV data Q4 FY19

column8: FAME II Launched — ₹10,000 Cr EV Subsidy
column9: Cabinet approved FAME II with ₹10,000 Cr allocation for 3 years (April 2019). ₹10,000/kWh subsidy for 4W EVs enabled Tata Nexon EV launch at ₹13.99L. EV 4W registrations tripled in FY20. AP benefits from this plus its own concurrent road tax exemption (A23).
column10: PIB Feb 2019; MHI FAME-II notification; SMEV data FY20; Tata Motors investor call

column9: Rs 10k cr; PV incentives restricted to commercial/fleet
column10: MoHI

column9: COVID-19 national lockdown (Mar 23, 2020) closed all RTOs simultaneously. April 2020 near-zero registrations. Simultaneously, BS6 emission norms took effect April 1, 2020, ending BS4 vehicle sales. March 2020 saw front-loading rush (BS4 sell-off). This is the highest-magnitude anomaly in the entire Vahan dataset. The subsequent months will show artificially high YoY growth due to near-zero base.
column10: MHA lockdown order Mar 23 2020; SC BS6 mandate; SIAM Apr 2020; FADA retail data

column9: BS6 emission norms mandatory from April 1, 2020 (Supreme Court order). Dealers and OEMs rushed to clear BS4 inventory in March 2020 — creating a front-load spike. April 2020 registrations then cliffed due to both BS6 transition AND COVID lockdown simultaneously.
column10: SC order Sep 2017; MoRTH BS6 notification; FADA Mar 2020 retail data

column9: National lockdown; April 2020 registrations near zero
column10: GoI

column9: Maruti Suzuki (45%+ of market) decided not to develop BS6-compliant diesel engines, exiting diesel entirely from April 2020. Diesel count for Maruti in Maker-dimension data drops to exactly zero from this date and never recovers. This is a structural, permanent event. Combined with Honda's simultaneous diesel exit, removed ~50% of diesel volume from the market immediately.
column10: Maruti annual report FY20; Maruti press statement Apr 2020; SIAM

column9: Diesel small-car exits (e.g. Maruti diesel discontinued)
column10: MoRTH

column9: Phased unlock from June 1, 2020. Pent-up demand + COVID-driven preference shift toward personal mobility (away from public transport) drove a sharp V-shaped recovery. June–September 2020 registrations above pre-COVID trend in several segments. Note: high YoY growth in these months is misleading due to near-zero April 2020 base.
column10: MHA unlock guidelines Jun 2020; FADA Jun-Sep 2020; SIAM

column9: Purchase incentives + road tax & registration fee waiver
column10: GNCTD

column9: Road tax + registration exemption for first buyers
column10: GoTS

column8: Diesel 4W Structural Decline — Post BS6 Premium
column9: BS6 raised diesel variant prices by ₹1–2L vs petrol (higher compliance cost). Combined with Maruti and Honda exiting diesel (April 2020), diesel share of PV registrations fell from ~40% in FY18 to ~16% by FY23. This is a multi-year TREND CHANGE, not a single-event anomaly. Annotate as a structural inflection starting FY2021 onwards.
column10: SIAM fuel-type data FY18-FY23; JATO Dynamics; Maruti annual reports

column9: COVID second wave (Delta variant) triggered state-level lockdowns Apr–May 2021. Less severe than 2020 (RTOs partially functional). All segments show 20–30% dip vs trend. AP shows comparable signal to national. Recovery faster than 2020.
column10: State lockdown orders Apr 2021; SIAM May 2021; FADA

column9: Regional lockdowns; supply + demand shock
column10: GoI

column9: State-level green taxes on older vehicles (>8yr for commercial, >15yr for personal) introduced from 2021. Accelerated replacement demand for old Petrol and Diesel vehicles. Context for why older vehicle registrations decline post-2021.
column10: MoRTH green tax notification 2021; State transport departments

column8: Global Semiconductor Shortage — OEM Supply Cuts
column9: Global semiconductor chip shortage hit India OEMs from mid-2021. Maruti suspended production. Waiting periods for popular models extended to 6–12 months. Supply, not demand, was the binding constraint. Registration data understates true demand — retail waitlists were at all-time highs while Vahan registrations were suppressed by supply. Eased from Q4 2022.
column10: Maruti investor call Jul 2021; SIAM supply alert; MHI supply chain report; Hyundai Kia supply advisory

column9: MHI reduced FAME II subsidy for 2-wheelers from 40% to 15% of ex-factory price (June 2021). EV 2W prices effectively rose ₹20,000–30,000. Brief demand adjustment dip visible in EV 2W chart. Recovery as OEMs absorbed costs.
column10: MHI circular Jun 2021; SMEV response; SIAM monthly EV data

column9: Post-second wave pent-up demand converged with semiconductor shortage easing (partial) and personal mobility preference shift to drive above-trend registrations Q2 FY22 through FY23. AP rural 2W showed particularly strong recovery. YoY growth rates inflated by low-base effects from Q1 FY21 — absolute volume vs FY20 trend is the correct comparison.
column10: SIAM FY22-23 data; FADA; RBI consumer survey

column9: Per-kWh purchase subsidy; registration incentives
column10: GoG

column9: Purchase incentives + road tax exemption
column10: GoM

column9: Mahindra launched new-generation Thar (Oct 2020), XUV700 (Oct 2021) and Scorpio N (Jul 2022) — three consecutive blockbuster launches. Mahindra market share jumped from ~5% to ~10%+ in 18 months. Visible as OEM-level spike in Maker-dimension data. Waiting periods of 12–18 months indicate genuine demand exceeding supply.
column10: Mahindra investor calls; SIAM monthly Mahindra data; FADA

column8: Ola Electric S1 Launch — EV 2W Mass Market
column9: Ola Electric S1 launched September 2021 at ₹99,999. First mass-market, sub-₹1L electric scooter from a credible brand. EV 2W monthly registrations doubled within 3 months of launch. Triggered competitive response from Hero, TVS, Bajaj — accelerating the entire EV 2W segment.
column10: Ola Electric press release Sep 2021; VAHAN EV 2W data Oct-Dec 2021; SMEV

column9: Production cuts; order backlogs especially PV
column10: SIAM/industry

column9: Rs 5/10 cut; several states cut VAT
column10: MoF

column9: Russia-Ukraine war (Feb 2022) caused global fuel price spike. India CNG price rose from ₹43/kg to ₹80/kg in Delhi (81% increase, Apr–Oct 2022). This temporarily eroded CNG's operating cost advantage. CNG vehicle registration GROWTH RATE halved — not absolute decline. Diesel also suppressed. EV comparatively benefitted. CNG resumed growth as it remained cheaper in total cost of ownership.
column10: IGL price notifications 2022; PPAC data; SIAM monthly data Jul-Oct 2022

column8: FAME II Fraud Crackdown — EV 2W Disruption
column9: MHI investigation found Okinawa, Hero Electric, Ola Electric and others violated localisation norms and claimed FAME II subsidies fraudulently. Subsidy payments withheld from top-5 e2W OEMs. EV 2W registrations plateaued/dipped May–July 2022. This is a supply-payment disruption, not demand cooling. Recovery once compliance confirmed.
column10: MHI investigation circular Apr 2022; SMEV statement; PIB May 2022

column8: CNG Price Hike Peak — Growth Rate Halved
column9: CNG price rose 81% in 18 months (₹43→₹80/kg in Delhi, Apr 2021–Jun 2022). CNG vehicle new registrations growth rate decelerated — not absolute decline, but rate halved. Part of broader Russia-Ukraine fuel shock cluster. CNG's total cost advantage vs petrol was temporarily compressed. Growth resumed as CNG remained cheaper long-term.
column10: IGL/MGL price notifications 2022; PPAC data; SIAM CNG monthly

column9: CNG prices spike ~50% (Russia-Ukraine); dented CNG economics
column10: PPAC

column9: Rs 8/6 cut on petrol/diesel
column10: MoF

column8: RBI Rate Hike Cycle +250bps — 2W Financing Impact
column9: RBI raised repo rate by 250bps (May 2022–Feb 2023) to combat inflation. Auto loan EMIs rose 15–20%. Most impacted: 2W Petrol (entry-level, credit-sensitive). 4W also affected but premium segment less so. AP rural 2W shows stronger signal due to higher NBFC dependence.
column10: RBI monetary policy May 2022-Feb 2023; SIAM 2W data H2 FY23; FADA

column8: Strong Hybrid EV Market Birth — Grand Vitara
column9: Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara with Strong Hybrid launched September 2022 alongside Toyota Urban Cruiser Hyryder. First mass-market strong hybrid at ₹15L price point. Strong Hybrid EV category in Vahan data goes from near-zero to material volumes from this month. The +400% YoY growth rate in Oct 2022 is misleading due to near-zero base — absolute volume is what matters.
column10: Maruti press release Sep 2022; SIAM Strong Hybrid monthly data; VAHAN Oct 2022

column9: SGST reimbursement + subsidies
column10: GoR

column9: Global semiconductor shortage eased from Q4 2022. OEM production normalised. Waiting periods for popular models (Creta, Nexon, Brezza) compressed from 12 months to 4–8 weeks. Registration volumes jumped as suppressed backlog orders were fulfilled. This is a supply recovery, not organic demand growth — the underlying demand had been waiting.
column10: SIAM Q4 FY23 supply data; OEM investor calls Q3 FY23; ACMA

column9: 100% road tax + registration exemption 3y (5y for UP-made)
column10: GoUP

column8: BS6 Phase 2 OBD2 — Diesel HCV Front-load
column9: BS6 Phase 2 (OBD2 + RDE requirements) took effect April 2023, primarily affecting heavy commercial vehicles (HCV). Trucking companies and fleet operators front-loaded diesel HCV purchases in Q3/Q4 FY23. Mainly visible in Diesel CV data, minimal impact on passenger vehicle registrations.
column10: MoRTH BS6 Phase 2 notification; SIAM HCV data Q3-Q4 FY23; FADA CV data

column9: Road tax exemption till Dec 2025 + manufacturing focus
column10: GoTN

column9: Real-driving-emissions norms; price increases esp. diesel
column10: MoRTH

column9: Andhra Pradesh launched EV Policy 4.0 (2024–2029) building on 2018–2023 policy. Continued road tax exemption for EVs plus additional incentives for commercial EV operators. Signal visible in AP EV data from 2024 onwards. TDP government maintained and expanded EV incentives.
column10: AP Govt EV Policy 4.0 notification 2024; EV Reporter AP analysis

column8: PM E-SEWA — EV 3W for Women Micro-Entrepreneurs
column9: PM E-SEWA scheme (2024) provided EVs to women micro-entrepreneurs, specifically EV 3-wheelers. Niche segment stimulation — mainly visible in EV 3W category. Small absolute volumes but represents structural government-backed demand.
column10: PIB 2024; MHI PM E-SEWA notification

column9: FAME II subsidy scheme ended March 2024. Classic front-load: EV 2W registrations elevated in February–March 2024 before expiry. April 2024 cliff as subsidy removed (prices effectively rose ₹1–2L). EMPS 2024 bridged the gap from April 2024 onward.
column10: MHI FAME-II closure notification; VAHAN Apr 2024 data; SMEV statement

column9: Electric Mobility Promotion Scheme 2024 launched April 2024 to bridge FAME II expiry gap. Covered EV 2W and 3W with reduced per-vehicle incentive. Prevented a full demand cliff after FAME II ended. Extended to September 2024.
column10: MHI EMPS notification Apr 2024; PIB; SMEV

column9: Interim e-mobility scheme between FAME-II and PM E-DRIVE (2W/3W only)
column10: MoHI

column9: AP state elections May 2024: YSRCP replaced by TDP-led NDA alliance. Government fleet procurement paused during transition. TDP reinstated Amaravati as capital city (Jun 2024) — CV (construction vehicles) and infrastructure demand revival. Temporary Q2 2024 all-segment dip followed by recovery. EV Bus procurement under AP EV Policy accelerated under new government.
column10: AP election results May 2024; AP Cabinet order Jun 2024 Amaravati; FADA AP monthly

column9: PM Electric Drive Revolution in Innovative Vehicle Enhancement scheme approved October 2024 with ₹10,900 Cr allocation. Covers EV 2W, 3W, buses with ₹13,000–15,000/kWh subsidy for 4W EVs. Stronger signal than EMPS — larger budget, longer horizon.
column10: PIB Oct 2024; MHI PM e-DRIVE notification; SMEV

column9: Rs 10.9k cr; no private e-car purchase subsidy; charging infra push
column10: MoHI

column9: GST Council's comprehensive rate rationalisation (July 2025) reduced effective GST burden on vehicles. All segments benefitted. Demand stimulation visible in Q2–Q4 2025 registrations. Effect strongest in mid-size segment where the cess reduction was largest.
column10: GST Council Jul 2025; PIB; SIAM post-GST 2.0 data

column9: Small cars moved to 18% slab (from 28%+cess); large cars 40% flat
column10: GST Council
